takeaways: Senior housing M&A heats up: NHI closed a $560M portfolio sale and a Health Care REIT entered senior living with a $425M luxury 3-property buy — capital is rotating into the space as boomers turn 80.
CRE debt is reopening: Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac each got $88B multifamily caps (up 20%) and the MBA forecasts a 24% jump in CRE lending volume, with cap rates poised to compress 5–15 bps.
Occupancy is set to top 90% in 2026 — the highest in NIC's 20-year tracking history — while 2025 inventory growth was just 1%, the lowest on record, tightening pricing power for operators.

items.headline: NHI narrows SHOP focus after $560M National HealthCare portfolio sale
items.summary: National Health Investors closed a $560M sale of a National HealthCare Corporation portfolio in Q1 2026 and has already deployed $212M+ in new senior housing investments year-to-date. The pivot signals a clean rotation toward the operating segment as demand fundamentals tighten.

items.headline: Health Care REIT enters senior living with $425M luxury acquisition of 3 communities
items.summary: A health care REIT made its debut in the senior living industry with a $425M purchase of three luxury communities, underscoring renewed institutional capital appetite for high-end senior housing assets. The deal validates the luxury segment thesis as boomers turn 80 in 2026.

items.headline: Senior housing occupancy set to exceed 90% — highest in 20 years of NIC data
items.summary: Average senior housing occupancy is projected to top 90% in 2026, the highest on record since NIC MAP began tracking in 2006. With 2025 inventory growth at just 1% (also a record low), operators face a structural supply-demand imbalance favoring rate growth.

items.headline: Brookdale's transformation rolls on as 2026 'selling season' nears
items.summary: Brookdale Senior Living is continuing its leaner-operator transformation and remains the third-largest owner of senior living real estate in 2026. With a strong start to the year, analysts are pointing to a potential 'selling season' for non-core assets — relevant for buyers like Lloyd Jones.

items.headline: Fannie & Freddie multifamily caps jump 20% to $88B each, MBA sees 24% CRE lending boost
items.summary: Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac each received $88B multifamily lending caps for 2026, up 20% YoY, while the Mortgage Bankers Association forecasts a 24% increase in overall CRE lending volume. Debt capital is meaningfully more accessible for senior housing and multifamily borrowers.

items.headline: Colliers forecasts 15–20% jump in CRE sales volume as cross-border capital returns
items.summary: Colliers projects CRE sales volume will rise 15–20% in 2026 as institutional and cross-border capital re-enters the market. Confidence is building with rates moderating and leasing fundamentals stabilizing — a constructive backdrop for transactions and recapitalizations.

items.headline: Commercial mortgage rates range 4.97%–12.75% in May 2026
items.summary: As of May 8, 2026, commercial loan rates run 4.97%–12.75%, with conventional CRE mortgage rates at 5.23%–8.75% depending on property type and credit. Cap rates are expected to compress 5–15 bps across most property types as the rate environment stabilizes.

items.headline: PE capital reopens for senior living after years on the sidelines
items.summary: PERE reports that senior living discounts are vanishing in the US in 2026 as private equity and institutional capital re-engage. Buyers chasing the boomer demand wave are bidding more aggressively, narrowing the basis advantage for legacy holders.

items.headline: Agentic AI emerges as the next phase of proptech in 2026
items.summary: Agentic AI is automating complex real estate workflows — procurement, pricing, inventory, event programming — and reshaping operations and competitive strategy. Operators that deploy agents early gain meaningful margin and resident-experience advantages.

items.headline: Propurti launches AI-powered property management platform today
items.summary: Edmonton-based Propurti Geeks officially launched its AI property management platform on May 9, 2026 at MacEwan University. The system covers leasing, rent collection, maintenance, compliance and financial reporting — another sign that AI ops platforms are crossing from pilot to production.

items.headline: Proptech turns AI on AI-driven rental and mortgage fraud
items.summary: Rental and mortgage fraud is surging — partly because of generative AI — and proptech vendors are racing to deploy AI-based identity, income and document verification. Senior housing operators with high-touch admissions should evaluate counter-fraud tooling now.

items.headline: Proptech 'monster rounds' return as investors pay premiums for scale leaders
items.summary: High valuations and large funding rounds are reappearing in proptech, with investors paying premium prices for category leaders with proven distribution. Capital is concentrating in winners — narrowing the field for adopters choosing long-term platforms.

items.headline: Big Tech Q1: Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, Microsoft post double-digit growth on AI capex
items.summary: All four hyperscalers reported double-digit revenue growth alongside tens of billions in incremental AI infrastructure spend. The AI capex race signals durable demand for data centers and power — and continued downstream pressure on knowledge-worker productivity expectations.

items.headline: 71% of leaders report higher stress; 40% considering leaving their jobs
items.summary: DDI's 2026 leadership survey finds 71% of leaders under increased stress and 40% considering quitting. Leadership development is HR's top priority heading into 2026 — operators should double down on bench strength and middle-manager support.

items.headline: US layoffs surge to 10-year high as orgs flatten hierarchies
items.summary: Layoffs in the US have hit their highest point in a decade as economic pressure and AI-driven efficiencies flatten leadership layers. By 2030, most organizations are expected to have fewer layers with leadership dispersing laterally across teams.

items.headline: PayPal's Lores moves to cut costs and jobs to defend fintech position
items.summary: PayPal CEO Enrique Lores is planning further cost cuts and layoffs as the company defends its position against banks and big-tech competitors in financial services. The move underscores the broader 'lean operator' trend hitting even mature platforms.

items.headline: Deloitte: tech leaders shift from 'operators' to 'orchestrators'
items.summary: Deloitte's 2026 Global Technology Leadership Study finds CIOs and tech leaders moving from running systems to orchestrating AI-augmented teams and partner ecosystems. The mandate is integration and outcomes — not infrastructure.

items.headline: Wellhub: 89% of employees say wellbeing drives higher performance
items.summary: Wellhub's 2026 State of Work-Life-Wellness report shows 89% of employees link prioritizing wellbeing to better performance. For senior housing operators, the finding reinforces that resident wellness programming is also a frontline-staff retention play.

items.headline: AI wellness coaches and biomarker nutrition go mainstream in 2026
items.summary: Personalized wellness is shifting from gimmick to mainstream, powered by AI analytics, at-home biomarker tests, and wearables tracking far beyond steps and sleep. Senior living amenities programs that integrate biomarker-based nutrition can differentiate on outcomes.

items.headline: 'Emotional fitness' arrives as a new category alongside brain-health focus
items.summary: 2026 is introducing emotional fitness as a discrete wellness category — pairing cognitive performance with emotional resilience — while consumers increasingly target brain volume, white-matter integrity and neuroinflammation. Programming for memory care and IL alike will follow.
